title: Wandergrain Supply screen-print pre-flight: audit 4 supplied traveller tee vectors for separation-readiness, registration & spot-color count, emit a marked-up seps report + print-shop go/no-go note
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief "Travel-Themed T-Shirt Design": tees that "capture the spirit of modern travellers"; designs using "universal icons (passports, coordinates, skylines)" rather than location-specific cliches; delivered as "layered vector files (AI or EPS) plus high-resolution PNGs with transparent backgrounds. Please keep text editable"; and "sized and pre-flighted for screen print or DTG." REFRAMED to the analyze slot: instead of CREATING the art (from-scratch design, out of connector scope), the persona is a screen-print shop that already RECEIVED four such traveller graphics and needs an independent separation-readiness pre-flight AUDIT before burning screens -- honoring the brief's own "pre-flighted for screen print" / "transparent backgrounds" / "keep text editable" requirements by verifying exactly those conditions. Fictional IP-clean brand (Wandergrain Supply Co., "Modern Wanderer" line); no real trademarks or proprietary data.
one_line_ask: Pre-flight four supplied traveller-themed t-shirt vector graphics for screen-print separation readiness -- verify transparent backgrounds, count distinct spot colors per design, check for registration marks and thin-line survivability, vectorize/inspect to flag raster contamination -- and emit a marked-up per-design seps audit report with a go/no-go recommendation for the exposure room.
full_brief: Wandergrain Supply Co., a small-batch apparel screen-print shop, has just received four traveller-themed t-shirt graphics from a freelance designer for its new "Modern Wanderer" tee line (universal travel icons -- a passport-stamp badge, a latitude/longitude coordinate mark, a minimalist skyline, and a compass-rose crest). Before any of these hit the exposure room and burn screens, the shop needs an independent pre-flight / separation audit -- the designer swears the files are "pre-flighted for screen print," but the shop has been burned before by art that looked fine on screen and fell apart on the press. We are NOT redesigning anything and NOT producing final films; we need an honest QA report. For each of the four designs, please verify and document: (1) background integrity -- the art must sit on a genuinely transparent background with no stray off-white halo or leftover matte that would print as a phantom screen; (2) spot-color / ink count -- count the distinct flat spot colors in each design and flag any design that exceeds our 4-color manual-press limit, or that hides near-duplicate colors that should be merged to one screen; (3) registration & trap readiness -- check whether the art carries registration marks / crop guides and whether abutting colors are cleanly separable or will need a choke/trap note; (4) detail survivability -- flag any hairline strokes, tiny text, or fine detail below what a 156-mesh screen can hold, and confirm the "keep text editable" promise by checking whether type is still crisp vector or has been flattened to messy raster; (5) vector cleanliness -- confirm each design is truly vector (not a raster PNG masquerading as art) by test-vectorizing and comparing. Deliverables: a per-design marked-up preview of each graphic (so we can circle problems), plus a single written seps pre-flight report (a recipe/findings note) that lists, per design, the spot-color count, pass/fail on transparency, registration presence, minimum-detail concern, and a clear GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO verdict the exposure room can act on. Files supplied as an Illustrator-exported package: four graphics (three already flattened to PNG with transparency, one still a layered vector) plus the designer's own color-spec sheet. Keep everything spot-color / vector oriented -- no CMYK conversions, no re-coloring, no new artwork.

inputs.name: tee_passport_stamp_badge.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat 2-color screen-print-style t-shirt graphic: a circular vintage passport-stamp badge reading 'MODERN WANDERER' around the rim with a small airplane and coordinate ticks in the center, rendered in exactly two flat spot inks (a warm cream and a faded travel-red), hard edges, no gradients, on a fully transparent background. Slight retro distress texture. High-contrast flat raster PNG suitable for a screen-print pre-flight check.
inputs.realism_notes: Clean 2-color spot art on transparent bg -- the 'good' control that should pass; gemini-3-pro-image for crisp legible badge text and hard flat edges.

inputs.name: tee_coordinate_mark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat minimalist screen-print t-shirt graphic: a latitude/longitude coordinate crosshair with the numbers '40.7128 N / 74.0060 W' and a thin hairline compass ring, in what looks like a single ink but actually uses a near-duplicate pair of dark greys plus a true black (3 near-identical dark tones), hairline strokes about 0.4pt, some very small 6pt text, on a transparent background but with a faint off-white 1px halo left around the edges from a sloppy export. Flat raster PNG.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately problematic: near-duplicate greys that should merge to one screen, hairline strokes below mesh-holdable weight, tiny text, and a stray off-white halo -- this is the design the audit should flag FIX-FIRST; drives the transparency + ink-count + detail-survivability findings.

inputs.name: tee_city_skyline.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat screen-print-style t-shirt graphic of a generic minimalist city skyline silhouette at dusk, but rendered with a smooth multi-stop gradient sky (orange to purple) behind the black skyline and a soft drop shadow -- clearly NOT separation-ready spot art, more like a photo-style poster. On a transparent background. Photoreal-ish blended tones, no hard flat color breaks.
inputs.realism_notes: The 'trap' design: gradients + soft shadow = effectively unlimited tones, impossible on a 4-color manual press without halftone work; should draw a NO-GO / needs-reseparation verdict. gpt-image-2 for the blended gradient/shadow look.

inputs.name: tee_compass_rose_crest.ai.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat 3-color screen-print t-shirt crest: a compass rose inside a shield banner reading 'WANDERGRAIN' with small registration crosshair marks and corner crop ticks placed just outside the artwork, rendered in three clean flat spot inks (navy, cream, mustard), hard edges, editable-looking clean type, transparent background. Crisp flat vector-style raster PNG.
inputs.realism_notes: Represents the one still-layered vector design (name flags an .ai origin exported to PNG for preview); carries real registration marks + crop ticks so the audit can confirm registration presence and a clean 3-color count -- the strongest GO candidate.

inputs.name: wandergrain_color_spec.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns design,claimed_ink_count,claimed_colors,has_registration,notes describing the designer's own claims for the four traveller tee graphics (passport stamp badge, coordinate mark, city skyline, compass rose crest). Make the claims slightly optimistic/inaccurate -- e.g. the coordinate mark claims '1 color' when it really hides 3 near-duplicate tones, the skyline claims 'spot color ready' when it is actually gradient art -- so the audit's measured findings can be compared against the designer's claims.
inputs.realism_notes: The designer's self-reported spec sheet, deliberately over-claiming, so the audit produces a real claimed-vs-measured discrepancy table -- the core value of a pre-flight.

outputs.name: seps_preflight_report.txt
outputs.spec: A single written screen-print pre-flight / separation-readiness findings note (recipe note) covering all four designs: per-design measured spot-color/ink count vs the designer's claimed count, transparency PASS/FAIL, registration-mark presence, minimum-detail/thin-line concern, vector-vs-raster verdict, and a clear GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO recommendation the exposure room can act on.

outputs.name: marked_up_previews_x4
outputs.spec: Four inline/marked-up preview renders (one per design) used as the visual evidence backing the report -- each showing the design as inspected, with the audited region (halo edge, thin-line area, gradient zone, registration marks) isolated via selection/preview so problems can be circled during sign-off.

outputs.name: coordinate_mark_isolated_ink_masks
outputs.spec: Selection/preview masks isolating the near-duplicate dark tones in the coordinate-mark design, evidencing that its claimed '1 color' is actually 3 near-identical inks that must merge to one screen.

outputs.name: compass_crest_vector_trace.svg
outputs.spec: A test-vectorization SVG of the compass-rose crest, used only as inspection evidence that the design is genuinely clean flat spot art with holdable edges (confirms the GO verdict); not a redesign or final film.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session to bring the designer's four supplied tee graphics and the color-spec CSV into CC storage for auditing.
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs for the four graphics and the spec sheet that every inspection step will reference.
Preview/read the designer's color_spec CSV so the audit has the CLAIMED ink counts / registration claims to compare each measured finding against.
Inline-preview the passport-stamp badge to visually inspect background integrity and flat-color edges (the clean 2-color control).
Select the background region of the passport badge to test transparency integrity -- confirm no stray off-white matte remains that would print as a phantom screen.
Inline-preview the coordinate-mark design to inspect its claimed '1 color' against what is visibly present.
Select the coordinate-mark background edge to detect the faint off-white 1px halo left by the sloppy export (transparency FAIL evidence).
Select the dark linework of the coordinate mark to begin isolating its distinct tones.
Invert the selection to isolate and count the distinct dark inks -- evidences that the claimed single ink is actually 3 near-duplicate tones that must merge to one screen.
Apply a halftone probe at press LPI to the coordinate mark's hairline strokes / 6pt text to visualize whether the fine detail survives a 156-mesh screen or breaks up (detail-survivability finding).
Inline-preview the city-skyline design to inspect for flat spot color vs blended tones.
Select the sky region of the skyline to evidence the smooth multi-stop gradient + soft shadow -- proof it is continuous-tone poster art, not separation-ready spot art (NO-GO evidence).
Halftone-probe the gradient sky to show how many tones/screens it would actually require -- quantifies why the skyline blows past the 4-color manual-press limit.
Inline-preview the compass-rose crest to inspect its flat 3-color art and locate the registration crosshairs / crop ticks.
Select the registration crosshair / crop-tick marks on the crest to confirm registration presence (the strongest GO candidate).
Test-vectorize the crest to confirm it is genuinely clean flat spot art with holdable edges (vector-vs-raster PASS evidence); inspection only, not a redesign.
Test-vectorize the skyline to demonstrate the opposite: the gradient/shadow art traces into an unusable tangle of shapes, confirming it is NOT vector-clean spot art.
Create a CC folder to collect all marked-up previews, ink masks, halftone probes and vector traces as the audit's evidence bundle.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: audit_evidence_folder, passport_bg_selection, coordinate_ink_masks, coordinate_halftone_probe, skyline_halftone_probe, crest_regmark_selection, compass_crest_vector_trace.svg
connector_workflow.note: Copy every inspection artifact into the evidence folder so the report's findings are each backed by a viewable marked-up preview.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: spec_sheet_read, passport_bg_selection, coordinate_bg_halo_selection, coordinate_ink_masks, coordinate_halftone_probe, skyline_gradient_selection, skyline_halftone_probe, crest_regmark_selection, compass_crest_vector_trace.svg, skyline_vector_trace_probe
connector_workflow.note: Emit the written seps pre-flight report: per-design measured ink count vs the CSV's claimed count, transparency PASS/FAIL, registration presence, thin-line/detail concern, vector-vs-raster verdict, and a GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO recommendation for each of the four designs.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining across an inspection pipeline. The two supplied loads (four tee graphics + color-spec CSV) enter via asset_initialize_file_upload(1) to asset_finalize_file_upload(2), whose uploaded_asset_urns seed every downstream read. The CSV of designer claims is read at asset_preview_file(3) so measured findings can be compared to claimed values. Each of the four designs runs its own inspection sub-chain off the previews: passport preview(4) to bg-transparency select(5); coordinate preview(6) to halo-select(7), dark-tone select(8) to invert(9) isolating its hidden inks, and a halftone probe(10) on its fine detail; skyline preview(11) to gradient-select(12) to halftone probe(13) plus a vectorize probe(17); crest preview(14) to reg-mark select(15) to vectorize(16). All marked-up artifacts converge into a CC evidence folder created at asset_create_folders(18) and populated by asset_copy_assets(19). Finally local_write_recipe_note(20) consumes the spec read plus all nine inspection outputs to emit the single seps pre-flight report -- the report is derived purely from what the chain measured, not from anything outside it. Nothing is composed or generated; every step either inspects a supplied asset or records the finding.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon analyze/audit: 20 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools -- above the >=10 call / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate designs through tailored inspection sub-chains (transparency-halo detection, hidden-near-duplicate-ink isolation via select+invert, thin-line/halftone survivability probing, gradient-vs-spot detection, and vectorize-as-diagnostic) then reconciling every measured finding against the designer's over-claimed color-spec CSV to produce actionable GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO verdicts. It respects every hard limit: nothing is composed, re-colored, or generated; image_vectorize is used ONLY as a diagnostic (clean trace = pass, tangled trace = fail), not to author a new deliverable; image_apply_halftone and the image_select_* / image_invert_selection tools are used purely to INSPECT and produce marked-up evidence; no generative fill, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no background replacement. The only non-adobe step is the terminal local_write_recipe_note [L] that emits the findings report -- the correct analyze-task output.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative fill; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the connector never creates artwork -- it only inspects supplied graphics); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. image_vectorize is used strictly as a diagnostic trace to judge vector-cleanliness (a genuine screen-print pre-flight technique), not to synthesize a new deliverable. image_remove_background and image_fill_area are NOT used. document_render_* / merge_data_* are NOT used (no layout composed). All inspection is read-only over supplied assets.
reverify.notes: Verified against slot V07 (operation O7, family Vector & Print, output_modality vector, workflow_nature analyze, vertical fashion_apparel) and grounded verbatim in the real 'Travel-Themed T-Shirt Design' Freelancer.com brief. Counts confirmed: 20 connector_workflow steps -> tool_call_count=20; 10 distinct adobe-server tools -> distinct_adobe_tools=10; tools_used lists the 11 unique tool names (10 adobe + 1 local). Every tool is in the allowed palette (asset_* [C], image_select_by_prompt/image_invert_selection/image_apply_halftone/image_vectorize [C], local_write_recipe_note [L]). Each potentially-risky step stays in bounds: (a) image_select_by_prompt / image_invert_selection only SELECT regions to measure transparency halos and isolate near-duplicate inks -- no fill or pixel edit is applied. (b) image_apply_halftone is used as a survivability PROBE to visualize whether hairline detail / gradients hold at press LPI; the halftoned previews are evidence images in the report, not delivered final art, and honor the spot-color-only constraint (no CMYK conversion). (c) image_vectorize outputs are inspection traces (one clean = GO evidence, one tangled = NO-GO evidence), explicitly not a redesign or final film -- and satisfy the slot's vector output_modality. (d) asset_preview_file / asset_inline_preview are pure reads. (e) The terminal deliverable is a findings report (local_write_recipe_note [L]) plus marked-up preview evidence -- the correct output shape for an analyze task. Fictional IP-clean brand (Wandergrain Supply Co. / Modern Wanderer line); no real trademarks or proprietary data. CREATE-out-of-scope brief was correctly reframed to an in-scope pre-flight AUDIT.
